LULC Maps provide up-to-date land use and land cover information derived from Sentinel-2 optical satellite images using deep learning-based image segmentation. The dataset covers key coastal and port areas, primarily along the Baltic and North Seas, with classifications including infrastructure, water, vegetation, and industrial zones. This product supports monitoring of land cover changes, aids sustainable spatial planning, and enables detailed analysis of environmental impacts in dynamic port and coastal regions.

This layer shows a time series of the Low Tide line from 2022 to 2025.  The contour was derived from all the low-tide cloud-free Landsat and Sentinel-2 images available.

For each section of contour, the data available in the layer comprises:
* *id*: unique identifier of the line
* *date*: date of the low tide event
* *springs_tide_range*: the mean springs tidal range at this location according to the FES2022 tidal model
* *obs_tide_level*: observed tide level across the satellite images used (average)
* *images_in_composite*: number of satellite images used to create the composite for this line

Select the data values tab and click on a feature to view details

Normalised Difference Water Index.   The satellite images captured at low tide (bottom 30% of the springs tidal range) are used to compute a composite NDWI (Normalized Difference Water Index) image. The 70th percentile is taken when calculating the composite to give more weight to dry pixels.
Low water line data derived from Landsat and Sentinel-2 optical images coupled with tidal model data.
Base layer: Stadia Alidade Smooth Dark
True colour composite of 2021 Sentinel 2 data.
